signIn.tagline: Track your household net worth without itemising every transaction.

assets.title: Assets
assets.body: An overview of your assets is coming here. For now, choose a category from the menu.

liabilities.title: Liabilities
liabilities.body: An overview of your liabilities is coming here. For now, choose a category from the menu.

investments.title: Investments
investments.body: An overview of your investments is coming here. For now, choose a category from the menu.

list.noActive_one: No active {{nounPlural}}. {{count}} inactive {{noun}} hidden — tick "Show inactive {{nounPlural}}" to see it.
list.noActive_other: No active {{nounPlural}}. {{count}} inactive {{noun}} hidden — tick "Show inactive {{nounPlural}}" to see them.

snapshot.createDescription: Enter the month-end value. Currency is locked to the position's native currency ({{currency}}).
snapshot.editDescription: Update the amount, statement date, or description. The month can't be changed here.
snapshot.wrongMonthHint: Wrong month? It can't be edited — close this, delete the snapshot from the row menu (⋯), then record a new one in the correct month.
snapshot.deleteDescription: The {{month}} reading will be hidden from reports. To fix a wrong month, record a new snapshot in the correct month after deleting this one.
snapshot.revaluationHintAppreciate: 💡 Suggested {{amount}} — based on +{{magnitude}}% appreciation /yr × {{months}} mo from {{anchor}}.
snapshot.revaluationHintDepreciate: 💡 Suggested {{amount}} — based on −{{magnitude}}% depreciation /yr × {{months}} mo from {{anchor}}.

terminate.description: Mark this position closed, sold, or otherwise terminated. A terminated position stops counting toward net worth from its termination month onward. You can also reopen it by switching back to Active.

import.title: Import snapshots
import.description: Bulk-add monthly balances from a spreadsheet — handy for backfilling years of history at once.
import.step1Hint: Fill one row per month-end balance (currency defaults to {{currency}}). Open it in Google Sheets, LibreOffice, Numbers, or Excel; save as <bold>.xlsx</bold> (not CSV). You only need the months you actually have a figure for.
import.dropHint: Drag your .xlsx here, or choose a file below.
import.dropActive: Drop the file to select it.
import.invalidFile: That's not an .xlsx file. Fill in the template above and save it as .xlsx.
import.needsFixing_one: {{count}} row needs fixing — nothing was imported:
import.needsFixing_other: {{count}} rows need fixing — nothing was imported:
import.emptyPreview: No rows found in the file. Add some balances and re-upload.
import.preview: Ready: {{insert}} new, {{update}} updated. Nothing saved yet — press Import to confirm.

importCreate.title: Import a {{noun}}
importCreate.description: Create a brand-new {{noun}} from a workbook — its details and full snapshot history, in one step.
importCreate.formatHint: The workbook needs a Detail sheet and a Snapshots sheet. The easiest way to get the right format is to Export an existing {{noun}} and edit it.
importCreate.needsFixing_one: {{count}} thing needs fixing — nothing was created:
importCreate.needsFixing_other: {{count}} things need fixing — nothing was created:
importCreate.preview_one: Ready: a new {{noun}} with {{count}} snapshot. Nothing saved yet — press Create to confirm.
importCreate.preview_other: Ready: a new {{noun}} with {{count}} snapshots. Nothing saved yet — press Create to confirm.
